CP00-880 is a clean descriptor-only pack: implementation, tests, validators, and the generated contract are mutually consistent; the 40-unit coverage/deliverable math matches the manifest exactly; no real data or secrets (synthetic tenants only); and the read-only-Claude / Hermes-evidence-only / human-approval-for-runtime boundaries all hold. Inspection was performed via Read/Grep/Glob over source (summary-only correctly disallowed for this pack).

Verified supports for the pack's descriptor-only claim: (1) Implementation is descriptor-only end-to-end — interface.js/service.js/states.js/policies.js/model.js and registry section rows all carry explicit runtime_execution:false, writes_product_state:false, permission_decision_written:false, audit_event_written:false, runtime_receipt_emitted:false, real_client_data_included:false, credential_or_secret_included:false attestations, deep-frozen. (2) Coverage math is exact: COMMERCIAL_INTERFACE_ROWS has 20 entries, so M04 slice(11)=9, M05 full=20, M06 slice(0,11)=11 = 40; deliverable distribution (test 6, hermes_evidence 2, claude_review 2, implementation 18, contract 8, security_audit 4 = 40) matches the manifest unit-by-unit and COMMERCIAL_CP880_REQUIREMENTS. (3) Validators (validators.js:990-1113) rigorously check distribution/row drift, authority boundary, per-row no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data guards, interface invalid-request-denied + cross-tenant denial + denied-response omission + contract-test runtime-closed, Hermes no-receipt, Claude read-only, model registry, capabilities and safety gates; the validate script chains the full CP00-873→CP00-880 binding and re-runs coverage/descriptor/contract validators. (4) Tests in model.test.js and service.test.js assert exactly those invariants (binding, coverage, descriptor runtime-closed, contract projection current, invalid/denied/review-required interface tests). (5) No real data or secrets — only synthetic tenants (tenant-synthetic-alpha/beta); every credential/secret hit is a negative attestation or guard name. (6) Boundaries intact: Claude is read-only and not final approver across request/interface/packets; Hermes is evidence/control-plane only (emits_runtime_receipt:false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false); human approval is required for runtime opening, deferred to CP00-881; local validation does not claim enterprise trust. (7) Generated contract (commercial-readiness-contract.json) is the deterministic registry projection with current_pack/latest_pack CP00-880, latest_projection CommercialCp880InterfacePermissionFixtureTailDescriptor, and validation.valid:true. (8) Scope hygiene confirmed: changed-file-scope.json isolates the active pack files from unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook docs), and no active untracked implementation files were omitted.

Review-hardening posture is sound: the recorded claude-review-result.json shows 0-byte stdout / PENDING, but this is the honest pre-review placeholder (status pending_review, valid_review_receipts:[], exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run:false) — not invalid evidence passed off as valid. The auth preflight passed (exit 0, well-formed JSON). production_ready:false and runtime_ready:false are the correct expected values for a descriptor-only pack; no production_ready gate is weakened. blocks_goal_closeout is false: the pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim, and the expected human/enterprise-trust approval authority boundary lies outside this receipt.

No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
